name: work-with-pr
tagline: Automates a full git-worktree PR lifecycle: implement, push, wait for CI+Cubic review, then auto-merge and clean up.
verdict: Well-structured and actionable, with good habits (isolated worktrees, no force-push/force-remove, secret redaction in PR bodies). Main risk: it enables auto-merge into the shared 'dev' branch by default on every run and skips its Cubic review gate whenever quota is exhausted, so code can land without a human review checkpoint unless the user explicitly opts

highlights.summary: "Enabling auto-merge is the default - do it unless the user explicitly told you not to merge" (line 285) and `gh pr merge --auto --delete-branch` (line 290) merge into the shared 'dev' branch autonomo

No explicit negative-trigger guidance (e.g. when a change shouldn't go through worktree+PR+auto-merge, such as trivial doc tweaks or when the user wants review-only). Description and body only state p

Verification loop is explicitly unbounded ('The loop has no iteration cap - keep going until done', line 183) with no stated max-iteration or time-box fallback beyond the Cubic quota check, so a persi
